name: Lotame Data Solutions API
what_it_does: Data management platform (DMP) and audience solutions provider with REST API — offering first-party data activation, third-party audience enrichment, Panorama ID (cookieless identity), data onboarding, and cross-device identity resolution for publishers, advertisers, and agencies managing programmatic audience strategies.
use_calls: Building and activating audience segments combining first and third-party data via Lotame API
Implementing Panorama ID for cookieless cross-site identity resolution
Enriching customer data with Lotame's third-party behavioral and demographic data
Managing DMP audience taxonomies and segment hierarchies programmatically
Querying audience reach and overlap data for media planning and buying
use_cases: Building and activating audience segments combining first and third-party data via Lotame API
Implementing Panorama ID for cookieless cross-site identity resolution
Enriching customer data with Lotame's third-party behavioral and demographic data
Managing DMP audience taxonomies and segment hierarchies programmatically
Querying audience reach and overlap data for media planning and buying
not_for: Simple customer data management without advertising activation (use a CDP like Segment)
Small businesses with minimal first-party data — DMP ROI requires meaningful data scale
Identity resolution without advertising context (use TransUnion/Neustar for pure identity)
best_when: A publisher, agency, or enterprise advertiser needs a flexible DMP to combine first-party data with third-party enrichment and activate audiences across programmatic channels — especially with cookieless identity via Panorama ID.
avoid_when: You need a customer data platform for CRM/personalization (use Segment or mParticle), have minimal programmatic advertising, or primarily use walled garden platforms.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code and client credentials flows. Bearer token auth. Account and client-scoped access. No webhooks — must poll for batch job status. JavaScript SDK for publisher data collection. REST API for segment management, reporting, and data activation. Panorama ID tag for cookieless identity. Data transfer to DSPs via batch export or pixel-based synchronization.

pricing.paid_tiers: Platform fee + CPM for data marketplace; contact sales; $50K-200K+/year typical
pricing.notes: New York company. Founded 2006. Raised $75M+ total. Backed by Spring Lake Equity Partners (PE). Pioneer in data management platforms. Panorama ID is cookieless identity solution across 5,000+ global publishers. First-party data enablement is growing focus (DMP to CDP evolution). 4,000+ clients globally. Third-party data marketplace with Lotame Audience Exchange. Merging with Audience Science historical legacy.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per account; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for segments, audiences, profiles, and reporting. OAuth2 auth. Good but aging documentation — platform evolving from DMP to first-party data focus. No webhooks — polling for batch jobs. JavaScript SDK for data collection. Batch data export for large audience files. Panorama ID tag configuration in publisher setup.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Segment reads fully idempotent. Report reads idempotent by period. Segment creation not idempotent — check by name before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Third-party cookie deprecation affects core DMP value — Panorama ID is cookieless replacement
TCF2 consent required for EU data collection and activation
DMP → CDP transition: some features better served by modern CDPs for non-advertising use cases
Third-party data in Lotame Audience Exchange subject to seller consent requirements
Batch job processing for large data exports — plan for async data delivery

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, CCPA, TCF2 compliant. US and EU data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Consumer behavioral data is sensitive — GDPR and CCPA compliance required for processing. TCF2 consent chain for EU programmatic activation. Third-party data usage requires vendor consent under TCF2. Data marketplace purchases require seller data usage terms review.
